name: Embervale Settlement
identity: Geothermal labor haven where recovering crews, repair workers, and shift-boss families regroup between tower-expansion and quarry assignments.
approach: Steam veils, heated halls, pipeworks, and dormer rows gather around the warmth of underground springs.
inside: Bath halls, repair shops, ration kitchens, bunk compounds, and shift boards structure a life built around rotation, recovery, and rapid redeployment.
who_is_out_of_place: people who waste heat, food, or bed space
expected_behavior: honor bunk claims and ration order
clear debts before asking for shelter favors
faith: small heat-shrine observances and achievement toasts mark survival, endurance, and return to duty
seasonal_change: freeze and storm periods turn Embervale into a packed refuge, triage node, and labor rebalancing center.
law_context: beds, heat, and rations remain communal assets even when local families control access order
public_behavior_texture: People who belong here are usually workers, repair crews, haulers, and public conduct is read through expectations such as respect shift rotation, honor bunk claims and ration order.
visible_power_signals: Ordinary decisions are visibly shaped by regional quartermasters, shift-boss families, repair captains.
local_tension_surface: The place feels stable only until pressures such as overcrowding, sickness, heat-failure risk begin showing through daily routines.
differentiator: Embervale Settlement stands apart because its economy leans on labor recovery, repair services; public faith remains visible through small heat-shrine observances and achievement toasts mark survival, endurance, and return to duty; outsiders are noticed fastest when they resemble idlers, strangers without crew ties.
encounter_rhythm: Most first scenes in Embervale Settlement begin through worker haven rhythms: arrival, observation, minor correction, and only then deeper access.
